hook.text: Deployments treat pods as interchangeable. But databases need stable identities. Pod-0 must always be Pod-0.

buildup.text: StatefulSets give each pod a sticky identity: a stable hostname, ordered startup, and persistent storage per pod.

discovery.text: Pod names are predictable: mydb-0, mydb-1, mydb-2. They're created in order and deleted in reverse order.

twist.text: Each pod gets its own PersistentVolumeClaim. Delete and recreate the pod, and it reattaches to the same volume.

climax.text: Databases, message queues, and distributed caches use StatefulSets. Anything that needs stable identity and storage.

punchline.text: StatefulSets: stable names, ordered lifecycle, persistent storage per pod.

quiz.question: When should you use a StatefulSet instead of a Deployment?
options: When pods need stable identities and persistent storage
When you need the fastest possible startup time
